1	# Add new provider
2	
3	## `@ai-sdk/<provider>` vs 3rd party package
4	
5	Every provider is welcome to create a 3rd party package. We are happy to link to it from our documentation.
6	
7	If you would prefer a 1st party `@ai-sdk/<provider>` package, please create an issue first to discuss.
8	
9	## Example
10	
11	https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/8136/files
12	
13	## How
14	
15	1. Create new folder `packages/<provider>`
16	2. Set version in `packages/<provider>/package.json` to `0.0.0`
17	3. Create changeset for new package with `major`
18	4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts` depending on what model types the provider supports
19	5. Add documentation in `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/<last number + 10>-<provider>.mdx`
20	
21	See also [providers.md](providers.md)
22	
23	## When in pre-release mode
24	
25	If `main` is set up to publish `beta` releases, no further action is necessary. Just make sure not to backport it to the `vX.Y` stable branch since it will result in an npm version conflict once we exit pre-release mode on `main`
26

1	# Provider Development Notes
2	
3	## Provider Options Schemas
4	
5	Provider options schemas are user facing.
6	We want them to be as restrictive as possible, so that we have more flexibility with future changes and allow for meaningful `null` values.
7	
8	- use `.optional()` unless `null` is meaningful
9	
10	## Response Schemas
11	
12	Response schemas need to be flexible enough to deal with provider API changes that do not affect our processing
13	to prevent unnecessary breakages.
14	
15	- keep them minimal (no unused properties)
16	- use `.nullish()` instead of `.optional()`
17	
18	## Provider-Specific Model Options Types
19	
20	Types and Zod schemas for the provider specific model options follow the pattern `{Provider}{ModelType}Options`, e.g. `AnthropicLanguageModelOptions`.
21	If a provider has multiple implementations for the same model type, add a qualifier: `{Provider}{ModelType}{Qualifier}Options`, e.g. `OpenAILanguageModelChatOptions` and `OpenAILanguageModelResponsesOptions`.
22	If options apply provider-wide rather than to a specific model type, use `{Provider}ProviderOptions` instead, e.g. `GatewayProviderOptions`.
23	
24	- types are PascalCase, Zod schemas are camelCase (e.g. `openaiLanguageModelChatOptions`)
25	- types must be exported from the provider package, Zod schemas must not
26	
27	## Provider Method Names
28	
29	For the Provider v3 interface, we require fully specified names with a "Model" suffix, e.g. `languageModel(id)` or `imageModel(id)`. These help with clarity for both developers and agents.

1	# AI SDK - fal Provider
2	
3	The **[fal provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/fal)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs) contains image model support for the [fal.ai API](https://fal.ai/).
4	
5	## Setup
6	
7	The fal provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/fal` module. You can install it with
8	
9	```bash
10	npm i @ai-sdk/fal
11	```
12	
13	## Skill for Coding Agents
14	
15	If you use coding agents such as Claude Code or Cursor, we highly recommend adding the AI SDK skill to your repository:
16	
17	```shell
18	npx skills add vercel/ai
19	```
20	
21	## Provider Instance
22	
23	You can import the default provider instance `fal` from `@ai-sdk/fal`:
24	
25	```ts
26	import { fal } from '@ai-sdk/fal';
27	```
28	
29	## Image Generation Example
30	
31	```ts
32	import { fal } from '@ai-sdk/fal';
33	import { generateImage } from 'ai';
34	import fs from 'fs';
35	const { image } = await generateImage({
36	  model: fal.image('fal-ai/flux/schnell'),
37	  prompt: 'A cat wearing a intricate robe',
38	});
39	
40	const filename = `image-${Date.now()}.png`;
41	fs.writeFileSync(filename, image.uint8Array);
42	console.log(`Image saved to ${filename}`);
43	```
44	
45	## Additional Options
46	
47	If you want to pass additional inputs to the model besides the prompt, use the `providerOptions.fal` property:
48	
49	```ts
50	const { image } = await generateImage({
51	  model: fal.image('fal-ai/recraft-v3'),
52	  prompt: 'A cat wearing a intricate robe',
53	  size: '1920x1080',
54	  providerOptions: {
55	    fal: {
56	      style: 'digital_illustration',
57	    },
58	  },
59	});
60	```
61	
62	## Documentation
63	
64	Please check out the **[fal provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/fal)** for more information.
65

1	# AI SDK - Replicate Provider
2	
3	The **[Replicate provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/replicate)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs) contains image model support for the Replicate API.
4	
5	## Setup
6	
7	The Replicate provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/replicate` module. You can install it with
8	
9	```bash
10	npm i @ai-sdk/replicate
11	```
12	
13	## Skill for Coding Agents
14	
15	If you use coding agents such as Claude Code or Cursor, we highly recommend adding the AI SDK skill to your repository:
16	
17	```shell
18	npx skills add vercel/ai
19	```
20	
21	## Usage
22	
23	```ts
24	import { replicate } from '@ai-sdk/replicate';
25	import { generateImage } from 'ai';
26	
27	const { image } = await generateImage({
28	  model: replicate.image('black-forest-labs/flux-schnell'),
29	  prompt: 'The Loch Ness Monster getting a manicure',
30	});
31	
32	const filename = `image-${Date.now()}.png`;
33	fs.writeFileSync(filename, image.uint8Array);
34	console.log(`Image saved to ${filename}`);
35	```
36	
37	If you want to pass additional inputs to the model besides the prompt, use the `providerOptions.replicate` property:
38	
39	```ts
40	const { image } = await generateImage({
41	  model: replicate.image('recraft-ai/recraft-v3'),
42	  prompt: 'The Loch Ness Monster getting a manicure',
43	  size: '1365x1024',
44	  providerOptions: {
45	    replicate: {
46	      style: 'realistic_image',
47	    },
48	  },
49	});
50	```
51	
52	## Documentation
53	
54	Please check out the **[Replicate provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/replicate)** for more information.
55

## Summary: Image Generation Provider Interfaces in Vercel AI SDK

Based on my analysis of the Vercel AI SDK monorepo, here's a comprehensive guide to understanding and implementing image generation providers:

---

### **Core Interface: ImageModelV3** (Latest Version)

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3.ts`

A new image-only provider must implement the `ImageModelV3` interface with these **required properties**:

1. **`specificationVersion: 'v3'`** - Discriminator to identify the interface version
2. **`provider: string`** - Provider name for logging (e.g., "openai", "fal")
3. **`modelId: string`** - Provider-specific model ID for logging
4. **`maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | GetMaxImagesPerCallFunction`**
   - Fixed number: hard limit on images per call
   - `undefined`: use global default (1 image)
   - Function: async resolver that returns number or undefined based on modelId

5. **`doGenerate(options: ImageModelV3CallOptions): PromiseLike<{...}>`**
   - Main async method that performs image generation
   - Must return promise with: images, warnings, response metadata, optional usage, optional providerMetadata

---

### **Call Options: ImageModelV3CallOptions**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-call-options.ts`

Properties passed to `doGenerate()`:

```typescript
{
  prompt: string | undefined,      // Optional for some operations (e.g., upscaling)
  n: number,                        // Number of images to generate
  size: `${number}x${number}` | undefined,  // Format: "1024x1024"
  aspectRatio: `${number}:${number}` | undefined,  // Format: "16:9"
  seed: number | undefined,         // For reproducibility
  files: ImageModelV3File[] | undefined,  // For image editing/variations
  mask: ImageModelV3File | undefined,     // For inpainting
  providerOptions: SharedV3ProviderOptions,  // Provider-specific options
  abortSignal?: AbortSignal,       // For cancellation
  headers?: Record<string, string | undefined>  // Custom HTTP headers
}
```

---

### **File Type: ImageModelV3File**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-file.ts`

Can be either file-based or URL-based:

```typescript
{
  type: 'file',
  mediaType: string,               // e.g., "image/png"
  data: string | Uint8Array,       // Base64 string OR binary data
  providerOptions?: SharedV3ProviderMetadata
} | {
  type: 'url',
  url: string,                     // Remote image URL
  providerOptions?: SharedV3ProviderMetadata
}
```

---

### **doGenerate Return Type**

Must resolve with:

```typescript
{
  images: Array<string> | Array<Uint8Array>,  // No conversion (return as-is from API)
  warnings: Array<SharedV3Warning>,           // Feature support warnings
  response: {
    timestamp: Date,
    modelId: string,
    headers: Record<string, string> | undefined
  },
  usage?: ImageModelV3Usage,                  // Optional token info
  providerMetadata?: ImageModelV3ProviderMetadata  // Provider-specific data
}
```

---

### **Warnings: SharedV3Warning**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/provider/src/shared/v3/shared-v3-warning.ts`

Three types available:

```typescript
{ type: 'unsupported', feature: string, details?: string }    // Feature not supported
{ type: 'compatibility', feature: string, details?: string }  // Fallback mode used
{ type: 'other', message: string }                             // Generic warning
```

---

### **Provider Options: SharedV3ProviderOptions**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/provider/src/shared/v3/shared-v3-provider-options.ts`

```typescript
type SharedV3ProviderOptions = Record<string, JSONObject>;
```

Structure passed from SDK:
```typescript
{
  "providerName": {
    "setting1": value,
    "setting2": value
  }
}
```

Example for OpenAI:
```typescript
providerOptions: {
  "openai": {
    "style": "vivid",
    "quality": "hd"
  }
}
```

---

### **Usage Information: ImageModelV3Usage**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-usage.ts`

```typescript
{
  inputTokens: number | undefined,
  outputTokens: number | undefined,
  totalTokens: number | undefined
}
```

---

### **Provider Metadata: ImageModelV3ProviderMetadata**

Keyed by provider name, each contains an `images` array:

```typescript
type ImageModelV3ProviderMetadata = Record<
  string,
  {
    images: JSONArray;
  } & JSONValue
>;
```

Example from OpenAI implementation:
```typescript
{
  "openai": {
    "images": [
      {
        "revisedPrompt": "A detailed cat...",
        "created": 1699564200,
        "size": "1024x1024",
        "quality": "standard"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

---

### **Real-World Implementation Examples**

#### **OpenAI Implementation**
**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.ts`

- Uses `postJsonToApi` for text-to-image
- Uses `postFormDataToApi` for image edits (files + mask)
- Returns base64 encoded images (`b64_json`)
- Reports warnings for unsupported features (aspectRatio, seed)
- Distributes token details across multiple images

#### **FAL Implementation**
**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/fal/src/fal-image-model.ts`

- Converts AspectRatio to size objects (1:1 → square_hd, 16:9 → landscape_16_9)
- Downloads images from URLs and converts to Uint8Array
- Handles single vs multiple images via `useMultipleImages` provider option
- Uses `convertImageModelFileToDataUri` for file/URL conversion
- Returns NSFW detection metadata

---

### **From SDK to Provider: How generateImage() Works**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479369449-n5luzr/packages/ai/src/generate-image/generate-image.ts`

1. **Receives parameters:**
   - `model: ImageModel` (string | ImageModelV3 | ImageModelV2)
   - `prompt: GenerateImagePrompt` (string or {images, text, mask})
   - `n, size, aspectRatio, seed, providerOptions, maxRetries, abortSignal, headers`

2. **Normalizes prompt:** Converts `DataContent` to `ImageModelV3File[]` for files/mask

3. **Parallelizes calls:** If requesting 10 images and maxImagesPerCall is 4, makes 3 parallel calls

4. **Batches metadata:** Merges responses, warnings, usage from all calls

5. **Returns:** `GenerateImageResult` with images, warnings, responses, metadata, usage

---

### **Quick Checklist for New Image Provider**

- [ ] Implement `ImageModelV3` interface
- [ ] Set `specificationVersion = 'v3'`
- [ ] Define provider name and supported model IDs
- [ ] Implement `doGenerate()` method
- [ ] Handle `maxImagesPerCall` (number or function)
- [ ] Return images as-is (base64 string or Uint8Array, no conversion)
- [ ] Collect and return warnings for unsupported features
- [ ] Include response metadata (timestamp, modelId, headers)
- [ ] Optionally report token usage
- [ ] Optionally include provider-specific metadata
- [ ] Handle files/mask if supporting image editing
- [ ] Use provider-utils helpers (postJsonToApi, postFormDataToApi, etc.)

---

**Key Files Referenced:**
- Provider spec: `packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/`
- SDK consumer: `packages/ai/src/generate-image/generate-image.ts`
- Example providers: `packages/openai/src/image/`, `packages/fal/src/`
- Shared types: `packages/provider/src/shared/v3/`

## Comprehensive Analysis of Vercel AI SDK Provider Packages

input.content:
# Plan: Add `@ai-sdk/pixelart` Provider Package

## Context

The task is to create a brand-new fictional AI provider package `@ai-sdk/pixelart` in the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. This provider supports **image generation only** (no text, no chat, no embeddings). It exposes a `createPixelArt` factory function and supports three provider-specific image settings: `size`, `style` (`"pixel"` | `"retro"` | `"8bit"`), and `pixelDensity`. The base URL is `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`. The goal is to follow the identical package structure and conventions as existing providers (e.g., `@ai-sdk/fal`).

No existing provider packages are modified. Only new files are created.

---

## Critical Files to Reference

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `packages/fal/src/fal-provider.ts` | Provider factory pattern to clone |
| `packages/fal/src/fal-image-model.ts` | `ImageModelV3` implementation pattern |
| `packages/fal/src/fal-image-settings.ts` | Model ID type pattern |
| `packages/fal/src/fal-image-options.ts` | Zod schema + `InferSchema` export pattern |
| `packages/fal/src/index.ts` | Export pattern |
| `packages/fal/src/version.ts` | Version injection pattern |
| `packages/fal/package.json` | Package metadata template |
| `packages/fal/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config template |
| `packages/fal/tsconfig.build.json` | Build TS config template |
| `packages/fal/tsup.config.ts` | tsup bundler config template |
| `packages/fal/vitest.node.config.js` | Vitest Node config template |
| `packages/fal/vitest.edge.config.js` | Vitest Edge config template |
| `packages/fal/turbo.json` | Turbo task config template |
| `tsconfig.json` (root) | Root references list — must add `packages/pixelart` |

**Reused utilities** (from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`):
- `postJsonToApi` — HTTP POST with JSON body
- `createJsonResponseHandler` — parse success JSON response
- `createJsonErrorResponseHandler` — parse error responses
- `combineHeaders` — merge header objects
- `withoutTrailingSlash` — clean base URLs
- `withUserAgentSuffix` — add SDK version to User-Agent
- `loadApiKey` — load API key from param or env var
- `parseProviderOptions` — validate Zod schema against providerOptions
- `resolve` — resolve `Resolvable<T>` values
- `zodSchema`, `lazySchema`, `InferSchema` — schema helpers

**Interfaces** (from `@ai-sdk/provider`):
- `ImageModelV3` — the spec the image model class implements
- `ProviderV3` — the spec the provider object implements
- `NoSuchModelError` — thrown for unsupported model types
- `SharedV3Warning` — warning type for unsupported features

---

## Files to Create

All files go under `packages/pixelart/`.

### 1. `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts`
Identical boilerplate to `fal/src/version.ts` — exposes `VERSION` from the injected `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` build constant.

### 2. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts`
Defines the `PixelArtImageModelId` type (a union of known model strings + `string & {}`):
```ts
export type PixelArtImageModelId =
  | 'pixelart-v1'
  | 'pixelart-v2'
  | (string & {});
```

### 3. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-options.ts`
Defines the Zod schema for provider-specific options and exports the inferred type.  
Provider options (`providerOptions.pixelart.*`) supported:
- `style`: `z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional()`
- `pixelDensity`: `z.number().min(1).max(100).optional()`

```ts
import { InferSchema, lazySchema, zodSchema } from '@ai-sdk/provider-utils';
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

export const pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(
    z.object({
      style: z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional(),
      pixelDensity: z.number().min(1).max(100).optional(),
    })
  )
);

export type PixelArtImageModelOptions = InferSchema<
  typeof pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema
>;
```

### 4. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts`
Implements `ImageModelV3`. Key details:
- Internal config type: `PixelArtImageModelConfig` with `provider`, `baseURL`, `headers?`, `fetch?`, and `_internal.currentDate?`
- `specificationVersion = 'v3'`
- `maxImagesPerCall = 4` (the fictional API supports up to 4 images per call)
- `doGenerate()` logic:
  - Parse `size` into `{ width, height }` if provided; otherwise leave undefined (warn if `aspectRatio` provided since it's unsupported)
  - Parse `providerOptions` with `parseProviderOptions({ provider: 'pixelart', schema: pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema })`
  - Build request body: `{ prompt, n, size: { width, height }, style, pixel_density, seed }`
  - POST to `${baseURL}/images/generate` using `postJsonToApi`
  - Parse response with `createJsonResponseHandler(pixelArtImageResponseSchema)`
  - Return `{ images, warnings, response: { modelId, timestamp, headers }, providerMetadata: { pixelart: { images: [...] } } }`
- Response schema (`pixelArtImageResponseSchema`): expects `{ images: Array<{ url: string, width?: number, height?: number }> }`; however since this is a fictional provider returning base64 data, images are returned as base64 strings (`b64_json`) — keep it simple with `{ images: z.array(z.object({ b64_json: z.string() })) }`
- Error schema: `{ error: { message: string, code: string } }` via `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`
- Warnings: push `{ type: 'unsupported', feature: 'aspectRatio' }` if `aspectRatio` is provided (unsupported)

### 5. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts`
Implements the factory function and provider interface.
- `PixelArtProviderSettings`: `{ apiKey?, baseURL?, headers?, fetch? }`
- `PixelArtProvider extends ProviderV3`: exposes `image(modelId)` and `imageModel(modelId)` returning `ImageModelV3`; all other model types throw `NoSuchModelError`
- `createPixelArt(options?)`: sets `baseURL` from `options.baseURL ?? 'https://api.pixelart.ai/v1'`, loads API key using `loadApiKey({ apiKey: options.apiKey, environmentVariableName: 'PIXELART_API_KEY', description: 'PixelArt' })`, sets headers including `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>` + User-Agent suffix via `withUserAgentSuffix`
- `export const pixelart = createPixelArt()` — default singleton

### 6. `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts`
Exports:
```ts
export type { PixelArtProvider, PixelArtProviderSettings } from './pixelart-provider';
```

Defines the Zod schema for provider-specific options and exports the inferred type.  
Provider options (`providerOptions.pixelart.*`) supported:

- `pixelDensity`: `z.number().min(1).max(100).optional()`

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `@ai-sdk/pixelart` Provider Package

## Context

The task is to create a brand-new fictional AI provider package `@ai-sdk/pixelart` in the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. This provider supports **image generation only** (no text, no chat, no embeddings). It exposes a `createPixelArt` factory function and supports three provider-specific image settings: `size`, `style` (`"pixel"` | `"retro"` | `"8bit"`), and `pixelDensity`. The base URL is `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`. The goal is to follow the identical package structure and conventions as existing providers (e.g., `@ai-sdk/fal`).

No existing provider packages are modified. Only new files are created.

---

## Critical Files to Reference

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `packages/fal/src/fal-provider.ts` | Provider factory pattern to clone |
| `packages/fal/src/fal-image-model.ts` | `ImageModelV3` implementation pattern |
| `packages/fal/src/fal-image-settings.ts` | Model ID type pattern |
| `packages/fal/src/fal-image-options.ts` | Zod schema + `InferSchema` export pattern |
| `packages/fal/src/index.ts` | Export pattern |
| `packages/fal/src/version.ts` | Version injection pattern |
| `packages/fal/package.json` | Package metadata template |
| `packages/fal/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config template |
| `packages/fal/tsconfig.build.json` | Build TS config template |
| `packages/fal/tsup.config.ts` | tsup bundler config template |
| `packages/fal/vitest.node.config.js` | Vitest Node config template |
| `packages/fal/vitest.edge.config.js` | Vitest Edge config template |
| `packages/fal/turbo.json` | Turbo task config template |
| `tsconfig.json` (root) | Root references list — must add `packages/pixelart` |

**Reused utilities** (from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`):
- `postJsonToApi` — HTTP POST with JSON body
- `createJsonResponseHandler` — parse success JSON response
- `createJsonErrorResponseHandler` — parse error responses
- `combineHeaders` — merge header objects
- `withoutTrailingSlash` — clean base URLs
- `withUserAgentSuffix` — add SDK version to User-Agent
- `loadApiKey` — load API key from param or env var
- `parseProviderOptions` — validate Zod schema against providerOptions
- `resolve` — resolve `Resolvable<T>` values
- `zodSchema`, `lazySchema`, `InferSchema` — schema helpers

**Interfaces** (from `@ai-sdk/provider`):
- `ImageModelV3` — the spec the image model class implements
- `ProviderV3` — the spec the provider object implements
- `NoSuchModelError` — thrown for unsupported model types
- `SharedV3Warning` — warning type for unsupported features

---

## Files to Create

All files go under `packages/pixelart/`.

### 1. `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts`
Identical boilerplate to `fal/src/version.ts` — exposes `VERSION` from the injected `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` build constant.

### 2. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts`
Defines the `PixelArtImageModelId` type (a union of known model strings + `string & {}`):
```ts
export type PixelArtImageModelId =
  | 'pixelart-v1'
  | 'pixelart-v2'
  | (string & {});
```

### 3. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-options.ts`
Defines the Zod schema for provider-specific options and exports the inferred type.  
Provider options (`providerOptions.pixelart.*`) supported:
- `style`: `z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional()`
- `pixelDensity`: `z.number().min(1).max(100).optional()`

```ts
import { InferSchema, lazySchema, zodSchema } from '@ai-sdk/provider-utils';
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

export const pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(
    z.object({
      style: z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional(),
      pixelDensity: z.number().min(1).max(100).optional(),
    })
  )
);

export type PixelArtImageModelOptions = InferSchema<
  typeof pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema
>;
```

### 4. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts`
Implements `ImageModelV3`. Key details:
- Internal config type: `PixelArtImageModelConfig` with `provider`, `baseURL`, `headers?`, `fetch?`, and `_internal.currentDate?`
- `specificationVersion = 'v3'`
- `maxImagesPerCall = 4` (the fictional API supports up to 4 images per call)
- `doGenerate()` logic:
  - Parse `size` into `{ width, height }` if provided; otherwise leave undefined (warn if `aspectRatio` provided since it's unsupported)
  - Parse `providerOptions` with `parseProviderOptions({ provider: 'pixelart', schema: pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema })`
  - Build request body: `{ prompt, n, size: { width, height }, style, pixel_density, seed }`
  - POST to `${baseURL}/images/generate` using `postJsonToApi`
  - Parse response with `createJsonResponseHandler(pixelArtImageResponseSchema)`
  - Return `{ images, warnings, response: { modelId, timestamp, headers }, providerMetadata: { pixelart: { images: [...] } } }`
- Response schema (`pixelArtImageResponseSchema`): expects `{ images: Array<{ url: string, width?: number, height?: number }> }`; however since this is a fictional provider returning base64 data, images are returned as base64 strings (`b64_json`) — keep it simple with `{ images: z.array(z.object({ b64_json: z.string() })) }`
- Error schema: `{ error: { message: string, code: string } }` via `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`
- Warnings: push `{ type: 'unsupported', feature: 'aspectRatio' }` if `aspectRatio` is provided (unsupported)

### 5. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts`
Implements the factory function and provider interface.
- `PixelArtProviderSettings`: `{ apiKey?, baseURL?, headers?, fetch? }`
- `PixelArtProvider extends ProviderV3`: exposes `image(modelId)` and `imageModel(modelId)` returning `ImageModelV3`; all other model types throw `NoSuchModelError`
- `createPixelArt(options?)`: sets `baseURL` from `options.baseURL ?? 'https://api.pixelart.ai/v1'`, loads API key using `loadApiKey({ apiKey: options.apiKey, environmentVariableName: 'PIXELART_API_KEY', description: 'PixelArt' })`, sets headers including `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>` + User-Agent suffix via `withUserAgentSuffix`
- `export const pixelart = createPixelArt()` — default singleton

### 6. `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts`
Exports:
```ts
export type { PixelArtProvider, PixelArtProviderSettings } from './pixelart-provider';
export { createPixelArt, pixelart } from './pixelart-provider';
export type { PixelArtImageModelOptions } from './pixelart-image-options';
export type { PixelArtImageModelId } from './pixelart-image-settings';
export { VERSION } from './version';
```

### 7. `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts`
Vitest tests for `PixelArtImageModel.doGenerate()`:
- Uses `createTestServer` from `@ai-sdk/test-server/with-vitest`
- Mock server URL: `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1/images/generate`
- Test cases:
  - Passes `prompt`, `n`, `size`, `seed` correctly in request body
  - Maps `style` and `pixelDensity` from `providerOptions.pixelart`
  - Issues unsupported warning when `aspectRatio` is provided
  - Returns decoded images from `b64_json`
  - Includes correct `response` metadata (modelId, timestamp, headers)
  - Throws on API errors (test error response handler)

### 8. `packages/pixelart/package.json`
Mirrors `packages/fal/package.json` exactly but with:
- `"name": "@ai-sdk/pixelart"`
- `"version": "0.0.0"` (new package per contributing guide)
- Remove `prepack`/`postpack` scripts (no docs file)
- Same `scripts`, `exports`, `dependencies`, `devDependencies`, `peerDependencies`, `engines`, `publishConfig`

### 9. `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json`
Identical to `packages/fal/tsconfig.json`:
```json
{
  "extends": "./node_modules/@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "composite": true, "rootDir": "src", "outDir": "dist" },
  "exclude": ["dist", "build", "node_modules", "tsup.config.ts"],
  "references": [{ "path": "../provider" }, { "path": "../provider-utils" }]
}
```

### 10. `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json`
Identical to `packages/fal/tsconfig.build.json`:
```json
{ "extends": "./tsconfig.json", "compilerOptions": { "composite": false } }
```

### 11. `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts`
Identical to `packages/fal/tsup.config.ts` — single entry `src/index.ts`, formats `['cjs', 'esm']`, `dts: true`, `sourcemap: true`, injects `__PACKAGE_VERSION__`.

### 12. `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js`
Identical to `packages/fal/vitest.node.config.js` — `environment: 'node'`.

### 13. `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js`
Identical to `packages/fal/vitest.edge.config.js` — `environment: 'edge-runtime'`.

### 14. `packages/pixelart/turbo.json`
Identical to `packages/fal/turbo.json`:
```json
{ "extends": ["//"], "tasks": { "build": { "outputs": ["**/dist/**"] } } }
```

### 15. Root `tsconfig.json` — add reference
Add `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` in alphabetical order (between `packages/perplexity` and `packages/provider`).

---

## Implementation Steps (ordered)

1. Create `packages/pixelart/` directory structure
2. Write `src/version.ts`
3. Write `src/pixelart-image-settings.ts`
4. Write `src/pixelart-image-options.ts`
5. Write `src/pixelart-image-model.ts` (core logic)
6. Write `src/pixelart-provider.ts` (factory + provider)
7. Write `src/index.ts` (exports)
8. Write `src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts` (tests)
9. Write `package.json`
10. Write `tsconfig.json`
11. Write `tsconfig.build.json`
12. Write `tsup.config.ts`
13. Write `vitest.node.config.js`
14. Write `vitest.edge.config.js`
15. Write `turbo.json`
16. **Edit** root `tsconfig.json` — insert `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` between `packages/perplexity` and `packages/provider`

---

## Verification

```bash
# From workspace root - install and link workspace packages
pnpm install

# Build the new package
cd packages/pixelart && pnpm build

# Run tests
pnpm test:node
pnpm test:edge

# Full type check across entire monorepo
cd ../.. && pnpm type-check:full
```

All 14 files are in place, plus the root `tsconfig.json` was edited. Here's a summary of everything that was created:

## Implementation Complete

### New package: `packages/pixelart/` (14 files)

**Source files (`src/`)**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `version.ts` | `VERSION` constant — injected as `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` at build time |
| `pixelart-image-settings.ts` | `PixelArtImageModelId` type union (`'pixelart-v1' \| 'pixelart-v2' \| string & {}`) |
| `pixelart-image-options.ts` | Zod schema + `PixelArtImageModelOptions` type for `style` and `pixelDensity` |
| `pixelart-image-model.ts` | `PixelArtImageModel implements ImageModelV3` — POSTs to `/images/generate`, returns base64 images, warns on unsupported `aspectRatio` |
| `pixelart-provider.ts` | `createPixelArt()` factory + `PixelArtProvider` interface + `pixelart` singleton; all non-image model types throw `NoSuchModelError` |
| `index.ts` | Public API re-exports |
| `pixelart-image-model.test.ts` | 11 Vitest tests covering request body shaping, provider options, warnings, image output, metadata, and auth headers |

**Config files**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `package.json` | `@ai-sdk/pixelart` at `0.0.0`, workspace deps, dual CJS+ESM exports |
| `tsconfig.json` | Extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig`, references `../provider` + `../provider-utils` |
| `tsconfig.build.json` | Disables `composite` for tsup builds |
| `tsup.config.ts` | CJS + ESM + `.d.ts` build with `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` injection |
| `vitest.node.config.js` | Node environment test runner |
| `vitest.edge.config.js` | Edge-runtime test runner |
| `turbo.json` | Turbo `build` task |

### Modified file: root `tsconfig.json`
Added `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` alphabetically between `perplexity` and `provider`.
